narrative:
Trea Turner and Pete Crow-Armstrong headline today’s roster as our elite anchors, both projecting well north of 85 points with significant platoon advantages against Latz and Mikolas, respectively. Colt Keith is positioned for a breakout performance at the #3 spot; his 80-point projection is bolstered by a favorable matchup against Vásquez and a 30% SP skill multiplier. Kazuma Okamoto and Christian Yelich provide further high-floor stability, both benefiting from favorable platoon splits that should maximize our output in the runs and RBI categories.

We are navigating a few tactical concerns today, most notably with Yordan Alvarez, whose projection is suppressed by a difficult L/L matchup and a significant 23% negative SP skill adjustment. While Carson Benge faces a minor park penalty, his elite BvP history against Mitch Keller offers a compelling case for his inclusion despite the #8 slot disadvantage. Conversely, we are keeping a close watch on Logan O'Hoppe and Junior Caminero, both of whom carry efficiency risks due to low lineup placement and poor BvP metrics; these spots will remain under scrutiny for potential late-bench swaps if the early game flow stagnates.
